narratives.Description: Build the complete Veil MVP: wave-based Human Mode, AI Arena spectator mode, vector aesthetics, Newtonian physics, variant-weighted ramming, rebindable controls, sound, and persisted high scores.
narratives.Origin: Ingested from specification.txt; operator decisions locked 2026-06-20.
narratives.Acceptance: Runnable at 60 FPS. Human Mode: ship pick at start, bounded arena, wave progression with jittered enemy counts, random enemy variants, power-up spawns, alternate weapon types, game over + score on death, local high scores. AI Arena: all-AI battle, pause on winner, auto-restart after delay; toggle via Pause menu (on-screen) or bound key. Sound via pygame.mixer. Controls rebindable with Restore Defaults. Window title Veil, default 1280x768 resizable. requirements.txt present.
narratives.Overview: Single MVP scope. Includes sound, high scores, power-ups, and alternate weapon types. Late-wave arena obstacles scale difficulty. Deferred post-MVP: multiple arena sizes, mid-run hull swap.
narratives.Requirements:
FR-1: Ship class with polygon hull, position/velocity/angle/angular velocity/health/thrust/weapon cooldown.
FR-2: Three ship variants (Light Fighter, Balanced Interceptor, Heavy Gunship) with distinct stats.
FR-3: Newtonian physics with optional drag for controllability.
FR-4: Projectiles — fast-moving, limited lifetime, damage on hit.
FR-5: Collision + projectile damage; ramming damage variant-weighted (light: dangerous self-damage; heavy: viable ramming tactic).
FR-6: Ships destroyed at zero health with explosion particle effect.
FR-7: Particle system for engine trails, hit sparks, explosions.
FR-8: Human Mode — wave-based progression, increasing difficulty per wave.
FR-9: Enemy count per wave uses jitter scaled by wave/stage number (not fixed count).
FR-10: Enemies spawn as random variants every wave/spawn.
FR-11: Ship selection at game start — Light, Balanced, or Heavy.
FR-12: Human Mode death — game over screen with final score.
FR-13: High scores persisted locally across sessions (Human Mode).
FR-14: AI Arena Mode — all ships AI-controlled.
FR-15: AI Arena — battle until one ship remains; pause on winner; show stats; auto-restart after short delay.
FR-16: AI Arena on-screen toggle only in Pause menu; keyboard rebind for toggle may still work in-game.
FR-17: Seamless Human ↔ AI Arena mode switching when toggled.
FR-18: AI behaviors — chase/attack, evasion when damaged, personalities, lead-prediction aiming.
FR-19: Bounded arena (wall bounce, no wrap); spawn points at edges.
FR-20: Static arena obstacles in later waves as difficulty scales.
FR-21: Score from kills, survival time, and wave reached; health bars, wave number, mode indicator UI.
FR-22: Default controls per spec; all rebindable (keyboard incl. NumPad, mouse buttons, wheel); Pause → Controls; Restore Defaults; persist to local config.
FR-23: Visual effects — starfield, nebula haze, vector ships, projectile trails, explosion screen flash.
FR-24: Sound effects via pygame.mixer (engine, fire, hit, explosion, UI).
FR-25: Power-ups spawn in arena during waves — temporary boosts (e.g. fire-rate, shield, weapon upgrade); minimal vector visuals; AI may collect where applicable.
FR-26: At least one alternate weapon type beyond default primary (e.g. spread, rapid, or charged shot), obtainable via power-up and/or wave milestone.
FR-27: requirements.txt with pygame dependency.
NFR-1: 60 FPS with 8–12 ships + particles.
NFR-2: Python 3 + Pygame only.
NFR-3: Modular layout: main.py, ship.py, ai.py, particle.py, utils.py (+ controls/bindings as needed).
NFR-4: Tunable constants at top of files.
NFR-5: Default resolution 1280x768, window resizable; window title Veil.
narratives.Traces: FR-1 through FR-27, NFR-1 through NFR-5; operator decisions 2026-06-20.
narratives.OpenDecisions: None — all MVP decisions locked. Wave enemy-count formula and obstacle layout are implementation tuning (constants), not design blockers.

Three hull types; collision damage scales by variant.

Modular AI attachable to any ship.
